CandidateWedge.description:
A narrowest-thing-someone-would-pay-for-this-week, surfaced as an option.

Derived from the ranked clusters: each wedge kills a tight set of high-breadth
complaints for a specific segment. The engine proposes a few and does NOT
choose. ``scope`` carries the minimal/broad/lateral alternative-trio; breadth
fields live here (a segment has no coherent ``breadth_unit``).

cluster_ranks.description: Ranks of the InsightClusters this draws on.
cluster_ranks.title: Cluster Ranks

evidence.description: Backing evidence (refs into report.evidence) — the proof this fork is real.
evidence.title: Evidence

pain.description: The specific complaint it kills (echoes a cluster claim).
pain.title: Pain

rationale.description: Why this is a GENUINELY distinct path, not a synonym of another fork.
rationale.title: Rationale

segment_id.description: The SegmentChoice.id it serves, if segment-specific.
segment_id.title: Segment Id

signal.description: Confidence chip — a thin fork never reads as strong as a deep one.

CandidateWedge.title: CandidateWedge

CorpusStats.description:
Statistical aggregates across the relevant corpus.

Distinct from ExplorationReport (which is the funnel) — these are
distributions over the threads that survived triage and were synthesized.
CorpusStats.title: CorpusStats

CrossReference.description:
One verified link between a Reddit cluster and the web findings.

At the LLM-output boundary, the triangulator returns list-id-prefixed
indices ('cluster:3', 'web:7'). The triangulator validator strips the
prefix and enforces that cluster ids are valid InsightCluster ranks and
web ids are valid WebFinding finding_indexes before producing this
contract. Mixed-list refs are rejected.

agreement.description: How the streams relate on this claim.
agreement.title: Agreement

note.description: One-line synthesis: where they converge, or what the disagreement is.
note.title: Note

CrossReference.title: CrossReference

EvidenceRef.description:
A downstream pillar's pointer to one piece of upstream evidence.

Resolves against the source `DemandReport.evidence` list by `evidence_id`,
or against an `InsightCluster` by `cluster_rank` when `kind == "cluster"`
(clusters have no leaf id of their own).

evidence_id.description: Target ResolvedCitation/WebFinding/PriceEvidence id. Empty for a cluster ref.
evidence_id.title: Evidence Id

kind.description: Which evidence family this points at.
kind.title: Kind

EvidenceRef.title: EvidenceRef

ExplorationReport.description:
What the three-bucket triage actually produced for this run.

Surfaced in the report's corpus_shape banner. The honest funnel:
threads_pulled (raw archive) → threads_auto_{accepted,rejected} by
cosine triage → threads_classified by middle-bucket LLM →
threads_relevant (passed classifier) → threads_synthesized (post
embed-group dedup).

`noise_composition` is binned reason counts from the classifier — what
KIND of irrelevant content dominated the rejected pile.

dedup_merge_rate.description: Fraction of synthesis units collapsed by embed_group near-dup merging (1 - groups/units). Surfaces breadth-collapse: a high rate means the dedup cosine threshold is folding many comments into few groups, shrinking distinct-author/breadth counts → demand magnitude. None ⇒ synthesis dedup hasn't run for this report.
dedup_merge_rate.title: Dedup Merge Rate

false_reject_rate.description: Estimated fraction of the auto-rejected band the LLM classifier would have kept (sampled, not exhaustive). None ⇒ backstop not run (empty band / disabled / legacy report). A high value means auto_reject_pct is too aggressive — relevant threads are being discarded on rank alone.
false_reject_rate.title: False Reject Rate

false_reject_sample_size.description: How many auto-rejected threads the backstop sampled to estimate false_reject_rate. 0 ⇒ backstop not run.
false_reject_sample_size.title: False Reject Sample Size

hybrid_percentiles.description: Percentile bands (p10..p90) of the blended cosine + BM25 hybrid score that actually drives the bucketing. Empty for legacy reports that ran on cosine-only triage.
hybrid_percentiles.title: Hybrid Percentiles

ExplorationReport.title: ExplorationReport

Fork.description:
Which axis is held fixed while the other is optimized.

Carried forward for backwards compatibility with migrated reports.
New-shape briefs use the slot_plan field directly.
Fork.title: Fork

InsightCluster.description: One ranked consumer-insight theme with its evidence.

breadth_count.description: Source-neutral breadth = distinct authors + distinct domains (authorless web). The demand_score driver, so authored / web / mixed clusters rank comparably. Equals distinct_author_count for all-authored (Reddit/HN) clusters.
breadth_count.title: Breadth Count

breadth_unit.description: What breadth_count counts: 'authors' (all authored), 'domains' (all authorless web), or 'voices' (mixed).
breadth_unit.title: Breadth Unit

claim.description: One-line synthesized insight (what consumers want/feel/struggle with).
claim.title: Claim

demand_score.description: Ranking signal. Weights distinct-author breadth ABOVE single-post virality (50 authors x 2 upvotes outranks 1 author x 200 upvotes).
demand_score.title: Demand Score

demand_signals.description: Aggregated source-declared signal vector behind this cluster, summed across members and keyed by kind (e.g. {'upvotes': 312}). Surfaced so a report shows WHY the cluster scored — the de-Reddit'd successor to a lone upvote total. The spec-weighted sum of these feeds `demand_score` above breadth.
demand_signals.title: Demand Signals

distinct_author_count.description: Number of DISTINCT authors expressing this — the honest base rate, not mention count. Authored sources only (0 for authorless web; see breadth_count).
distinct_author_count.title: Distinct Author Count

mention_count.description: Total mentions (>= distinct_author_count). Kept separate so base-rate honesty is visible.
mention_count.title: Mention Count

quotes.description: 2-3 verified quotes (materialized, portable). A cluster with zero verified quotes is never shipped (no-quote-no-theme).
quotes.title: Quotes

source_mix.description: Source composition of the cluster's MEMBERS: source_id → count of members from that source (computed over ALL members, not just the 2-3 surfaced quotes, so it reflects true composition). A Reddit-only cluster is {'reddit': N}. Disclosure only — surfaced so a human can SEE when a theme is suspiciously cross-source; it never feeds demand_score or the verdict band (which stay breadth-only). Defaulted/empty so old payloads validate.
source_mix.title: Source Mix

InsightCluster.title: InsightCluster

ResearchBrief.description:
The contract produced by the conversational planner.

A Brief is the persisted, versioned record of what the planner and the
user agreed to research. Briefs are IMMUTABLE: editing a brief writes a
new row with a fresh `brief_id` and a `supersedes` pointer to the prior
version. Runs FK to a single brief_id; a re-run after edit creates a new
run pointing at the new brief.

brief_id.description: Stable UUID for this version of the brief.
brief_id.title: Brief Id

decision_context.description: What decision will this research inform.
decision_context.title: Decision Context

finalized_at.description: When the user confirmed the brief preview and kicked off the run.
finalized_at.title: Finalized At

must_address.description: Specific sub-questions the report MUST answer. Surfaced in must_address_resolution.
must_address.title: Must Address

question.description: The refined research question after the planner conversation.
question.title: Question

relevance_rubric.description: One-paragraph definition of relevance the exploration classifier follows. Goes in the user role of the classifier prompt, never system.
relevance_rubric.title: Relevance Rubric

success_criteria.description: How the user will judge if the report did its job.
success_criteria.title: Success Criteria

supersedes.description: Prior brief_id this version replaces. None for the first version.
supersedes.title: Supersedes

target_subreddits.description: Reddit communities the corpus pull will cover.
target_subreddits.title: Target Subreddits

time_window_months.description: How far back to pull from the historical corpus.
time_window_months.title: Time Window Months

version.description: Monotonic version counter within the supersedes chain.
version.title: Version

web_research_directions.description: External angles to pursue in the web stream.
web_research_directions.title: Web Research Directions

workspace_id.description: Owning tenant. Defaults to 'local' for library/CLI use.
workspace_id.title: Workspace Id

ResearchBrief.title: ResearchBrief

ResolvedCitation.description:
One verified, source-neutral quote backing a cluster — the PORTABLE form.

This is what serializes to ``runs/*.json``, ``research.md``, and every MCP
response. It carries the verbatim ``text`` + ``source_url`` INLINE so a
report stays readable when detached from the corpus (committed to git, sent
over the wire) — the portability guarantee.

`text` MUST exact-match the backing source comment (the quote-verification
gate) — the service rejects any quote that doesn't, so a ResolvedCitation
that reaches this contract is guaranteed real.

Generic over sources: it replaces the Reddit-shaped fields of the old
``QuoteCitation`` (``permalink`` → ``source_url``, ``subreddit`` → ``source``
(+ ``source_name`` / ``extra``), ``upvotes`` → ``engagement``).

author_hash.description: Stable pseudonymous author id (salted hash) — for distinct-author counting, never the raw username. Pseudonymization, not anonymization.
author_hash.title: Author Hash

engagement.description: Source-native engagement signal (Reddit upvotes, HN points, …), for context.
engagement.title: Engagement

evidence_id.description: Stable content-addressed citation id (``q:<hash of source_url|text>``). Defaulted/recomputed by the `id` computed field; carried so a CitationRef matches.
evidence_id.title: Evidence Id

extra.description: Source-specific fields that don't earn a spine column.
extra.title: Extra

record_id.description: Corpus-wide id of the backing record/comment, for live re-resolution.
record_id.title: Record Id

signals.description: Open, source-declared demand signals for this quote, keyed by kind (e.g. {'upvotes': 12}). The generalization of `engagement`: the deterministic scorer reads known kinds via the SignalSpec registry, unknown kinds are context.
signals.title: Signals

source_name.description: Human-readable origin label, e.g. 'r/Supplements' for Reddit.
source_name.title: Source Name

source_url.description: Resolvable link to the quote in context (the provenance link).
source_url.title: Source Url

text.description: Verbatim quote, exact-matched to the source comment.
text.title: Text

ResolvedCitation.title: ResolvedCitation

SegmentChoice.description:
A distinct audience you could build FOR — the demographic fork.

Supersedes the descriptive ``AudienceSegment`` (kept as an alias below):
same ``name`` / ``profile`` / scores, now decision-bearing. ``name`` stays
canonical (downstream readers depend on ``.name``; old payloads validate
directly). ``overlap`` lets a surface SUPPRESS a fake fork — two "segments"
that are ~the same authors are not a real choice.

evidence.description: Backing evidence (refs into report.evidence) — the proof this fork is real.
evidence.title: Evidence

overlap.description: id -> author-set Jaccard vs other segments; near 1.0 ⇒ NOT distinct.
overlap.title: Overlap

rationale.description: Why this is a GENUINELY distinct path, not a synonym of another fork.
rationale.title: Rationale

signal.description: Confidence chip — a thin fork never reads as strong as a deep one.

SegmentChoice.title: SegmentChoice

SignalStrength.description:
Per-cluster confidence, driven by distinct-author count.

Rendered as a text-labelled chip (never color alone) so a thin-evidence
cluster never reads as authoritative as a well-supported one.
SignalStrength.title: SignalStrength

SkippedSource.description:
A source the selector ranked but couldn't reach — its auth key is unset.

Carries the same ``env_var`` / ``fix`` shape as
:class:`~metalworks.errors.MissingKeyError` so a pre-flight line can tell the
operator exactly which variable to set to unlock the source on the next run.

env_var.description: The environment variable(s) to set to unlock it (MissingKeyError shape).
env_var.title: Env Var

fix.description: The actionable remediation line (e.g. 'Set the TRUSTPILOT_API_KEY ...').
fix.title: Fix

reason.description: Why it was skipped (e.g. 'no key' — its auth env var is unset).
reason.title: Reason

source_id.description: The registered source id that was skipped.
source_id.title: Source Id

SkippedSource.title: SkippedSource

source.description: Source-neutral origin label for this map row (e.g. 'r/Supplements').
source.title: Source

subreddit.description: Deprecated Reddit alias for `source`; kept additive so the pipeline's existing population still validates. Read `source`.
subreddit.title: Subreddit

SourceSelection.description:
The brief-aware source pick — surfaced so a run is never a silent black box.

``selected`` is the ordered, access-gated, relevance-ranked source id list the
run actually pulled from. ``skipped`` names sources the selector wanted but
couldn't reach (no key), each with its env var so the operator can unlock it.
``floor_applied`` is the non-removable-floor flag: when the gated ranking
yielded nothing the run fell back to ``reddit`` (or the configured default)
rather than producing an empty corpus, and ``caveat`` says so.

caveat.description: A distinct caveat when the floor was applied or a source was skipped.
caveat.title: Caveat

floor_applied.description: True ⇒ the gated ranking yielded nothing and the run fell back to the floor.
floor_applied.title: Floor Applied

rationale.description: One-line human rationale for the pick (selector reasoning).
rationale.title: Rationale

selected.description: Ordered source ids the run pulled from (access-gated, relevance-ranked).
selected.title: Selected

skipped.description: Sources the selector wanted but couldn't reach (no key).
skipped.title: Skipped

SourceSelection.title: SourceSelection

SynthesisThresholds.description:
Synthesis-stage cutoffs that shape how much evidence reaches the LLM.

Surfaced (issue #82) so the numbers that gate breadth/magnitude are visible
and overridable, not buried as module constants. Defaults preserve the prior
hard-coded behavior exactly.

- ``dedup_cosine_threshold`` (was ``embed_group.DEDUP_COSINE_THRESHOLD``):
  comments closer than this cosine are merged into one near-dup group, so the
  LLM labels distinct ideas instead of N paraphrases. It directly drives
  distinct-author / breadth counts → demand magnitude; too LOW collapses real
  breadth, too HIGH lets paraphrases inflate it.
- ``comment_cap`` (was ``loader.DEFAULT_COMMENT_CAP``): upper bound on comments
  loaded into synthesis, taken highest-engagement-first so the cap trims the
  long tail, not the signal.

comment_cap.description: Max comments loaded into synthesis (engagement-sorted, so the cap chops noise not signal).
comment_cap.title: Comment Cap

dedup_cosine_threshold.description: Cosine at/above which two comments are treated as near-duplicates and merged (feeds distinct-author/breadth counts → demand magnitude).
dedup_cosine_threshold.title: Dedup Cosine Threshold

SynthesisThresholds.title: SynthesisThresholds

TargetSubreddit.description:
A subreddit the planner suggested for this brief, with its rationale.

The rationale is what the planner showed the user during D5 ("Target
subreddits"). It's persisted so the report can cite back to the
planner's reasoning when the user reads the source_map.

rationale.description: One-line reason this sub was suggested.
rationale.title: Rationale

TargetSubreddit.title: TargetSubreddit

TriageThresholds.description:
Three-bucket cutoffs for the exploration step.

Top auto_accept_pct: high cosine to question → skip LLM, accept.
Bottom auto_reject_pct: low cosine → skip LLM, reject.
Middle: LLM classifier with confidence.

Gate on absolute cosine bands AND percentile. Both cosine_floor /
cosine_ceiling and the percentages are honored; if they disagree by >5%
relevance rate, the triage step alerts.

backstop_sample_size.description: How many auto-rejected threads to sample back through the LLM classifier to ESTIMATE the false-reject rate (the fraction the rank-only reject band wrongly discarded). 0 disables the backstop. The sample is classified but NOT promoted — this measures the threshold, it does not change the corpus.
backstop_sample_size.title: Backstop Sample Size

cosine_ceiling.description: Absolute cosine above which a thread is RESCUED from the auto-reject band back to the middle for the LLM to check, regardless of percentile. The recall safety valve: with `auto_reject_pct=0.50` half the corpus is rejected on rank alone, so a genuinely relevant thread mis-ranked into the bottom band would vanish unseen. 0.50 is a deliberately conservative non-None default — well above the cosine of off-topic noise, so it rescues the obvious mis-rankings without flooding the middle bucket; set None to restore the old percentile-only behavior.
cosine_ceiling.title: Cosine Ceiling

cosine_floor.description: Absolute cosine below which to auto-reject regardless of percentile. None = percentile-only.
cosine_floor.title: Cosine Floor

TriageThresholds.title: TriageThresholds

WebFinding.description:
One structured finding from the web research stream.

Indexed by `finding_index` (1-based). The triangulator references
findings by index — never by free text — preserving the structural-
provenance contract across both streams.

`source_url` / `source_title` / `published_at` come from the grounding
tool's citation metadata, not from the LLM. The LLM produces only
`claim` + `specifics` + index assignments.

claim.description: One-line factual claim from the web.
claim.title: Claim

finding_index.description: 1-based index within this report's web_findings list.
finding_index.title: Finding Index

published_at.description: From the grounding metadata when available.
published_at.title: Published At

source_title.description: From the grounding tool's citation metadata.
source_title.title: Source Title

source_url.description: From the grounding tool's citation metadata.
source_url.title: Source Url

specifics.description: The number, date, name, or quote that anchors the claim.
specifics.title: Specifics

WebFinding.title: WebFinding

description:
The full report — the canonical shape returned by every surface.

Optional / default-empty fields keep the additive-contract parity guard
passing (reports produced by earlier shapes validate against this).

brief.description: The brief this run was produced against (frozen-version FK).

chosen_segment_id.description: Set by a surface when the user picks a segment. None ⇒ engine surfaced the fork but nothing chosen yet; deterministic callers read `default_segment`.
chosen_segment_id.title: Chosen Segment Id

client_id.description: Owning tenant. Set by the service, never the LLM. Defaults to 'local' for library/CLI use.
client_id.title: Client Id

lineage_id.description: Stable id for this report's refresh lineage. Empty ⇒ first version (read `effective_lineage_id`, which falls back to `report_id`).
lineage_id.title: Lineage Id

optimized_axis.description: The fork's optimized axis when computed; None otherwise.
optimized_axis.title: Optimized Axis

parent_report_id.description: The prior version's `report_id` this refresh supersedes. None for v1.
parent_report_id.title: Parent Report Id

pinned_axis.description: The fork's pinned axis when computed; None otherwise.
pinned_axis.title: Pinned Axis

source.description: Provenance of the corpus this run drew on: 'reddit_arctic_shift' | 'reddit_live' | 'hackernews' | 'reviews' | 'mixed'.
source.title: Source

source_selection.description: The brief-aware source pick + skipped/floor rationale, when the selector ran. None ⇒ the run used the configured/default sources without the selector.

version.description: Monotonic version within the lineage (1 = original run).
version.title: Version

title: DemandReport
